tEn: Scaling Laws for Neural Language Models
tZh: 神经语言模型的缩放定律
sEn: Language model performance scales as a power-law with model size, dataset size, and compute, enabling optimal compute-efficient training with very large models on modest data.
sZh: 语言模型性能随模型大小、数据集大小和计算量呈幂律缩放，从而可以在适度的数据上训练非常大的模型，实现计算效率最优的训练。
absEn: We study empirical scaling laws for language model performance on the cross-entropy loss. The loss scales as a power-law with model size, dataset size, and the amount of compute used for training, with some trends spanning more than seven orders of magnitude. Other architectural details such as network width or depth have minimal effects within a wide range. Simple equations govern the dependence of overfitting on model/dataset size and the dependence of training speed on model size. These relationships allow us to determine the optimal allocation of a fixed compute budget. Larger models are significantly more sample-efficient, such that optimally compute-efficient training involves training very large models on a relatively modest amount of data and stopping significantly before convergence.
absZh: 我们研究了语言模型在交叉熵损失上的经验缩放定律。损失随模型大小、数据集大小以及用于训练的计算量呈幂律缩放，某些趋势跨越了七个数量级以上。其他架构细节，如网络宽度或深度，在广泛范围内影响甚微。简单的方程控制了过拟合对模型/数据集大小的依赖性以及训练速度对模型大小的依赖性。这些关系使我们能够确定固定计算预算的最优分配。更大的模型在样本效率上显著更高，因此计算效率最优的训练涉及在相对适中的数据量上训练非常大的模型，并在显著收敛之前停止。

contrib.en: Language model cross-entropy loss follows power-law scaling with model size, dataset size, and compute over seven orders of magnitude.
contrib.zh: 语言模型交叉熵损失随模型规模、数据集大小和算力呈幂律缩放，跨越七个数量级。

contrib.en: Optimal compute-efficient training uses very large models trained on modest data, stopping before convergence.
contrib.zh: 最优算力高效训练使用非常大的模型在适度数据上训练，并在收敛前停止。

contrib.en: Performance depends weakly on architectural details like depth or width compared to scale factors.
contrib.zh: 与规模因素相比，性能对深度或宽度等架构细节的依赖很弱。

contrib.en: Overfitting and training speed are governed by simple equations relating model size and dataset size.
contrib.zh: 过拟合和训练速度由模型大小和数据集大小的简单方程控制。

contrib.en: Larger models are more sample-efficient, requiring fewer data points to reach the same loss.
contrib.zh: 更大的模型样本效率更高，达到相同损失所需的数据点更少。

contrib.en: The critical batch size for training follows a power-law in the loss, independent of model size.
contrib.zh: 训练的关键批次大小随损失呈幂律变化，与模型大小无关。

limits.en: Scaling laws may break down at extreme scales due to non-zero entropy of natural language.
limits.zh: 由于自然语言的非零熵，缩放定律可能在极端规模下失效。

limits.en: Results are based on Transformer architecture; may not generalize to other architectures.
limits.zh: 结果基于Transformer架构，可能不适用于其他架构。

limits.en: The study uses a single dataset (WebText2); transfer to other distributions incurs a constant loss penalty.
limits.zh: 研究使用单一数据集（WebText2）；迁移到其他分布会带来恒定的损失惩罚。

limits.en: Optimal compute allocation assumes fixed batch size and may not hold for varying hardware constraints.
limits.zh: 最优算力分配假设固定批次大小，可能不适用于变化的硬件约束。

limits.en: The power-law fits are empirical and lack a theoretical derivation from first principles.
limits.zh: 幂律拟合是经验性的，缺乏第一性原理的理论推导。

paras.en: We study empirical scaling laws for language model performance on the cross-entropy loss. The loss scales as a power-law with model size, dataset size, and the amount of compute used for training, with some trends spanning more than seven orders of magnitude. Other architectural details such as network width or depth have minimal effects within a wide range. Simple equations govern the dependence of overfitting on model/dataset size and the dependence of training speed on model size. These relationships allow us to determine the optimal allocation of a fixed compute budget. Larger models are significantly more sample-efficient, such that optimally compute-efficient training involves training very large models on a relatively modest amount of data and stopping significantly before convergence.
paras.zh: 我们研究了语言模型在交叉熵损失上的经验缩放定律。损失随模型大小、数据集大小以及用于训练的算力量呈幂律缩放，某些趋势跨越超过七个数量级。其他架构细节如网络宽度或深度在很大范围内影响极小。简单的方程控制了过拟合对模型/数据集大小的依赖以及训练速度对模型大小的依赖。这些关系使我们能够确定固定算力预算的最优分配。更大的模型在样本效率上显著更高，因此最优算力效率的训练涉及在相对适中的数据量上训练非常大的模型，并在显著收敛之前停止。

paras.en: Language provides a natural domain for the study of artificial intelligence, as the vast majority of reasoning tasks can be efficiently expressed and evaluated in language, and the world’s text provides a wealth of data for unsupervised learning via generative modeling. Deep learning has recently seen rapid progress in language modeling, with state of the art models [RNSS18, DCLT18, YDY+19, LOG+19, RSR+19] approaching human-level performance on many specific tasks [WPN+19], including the composition of coherent multi-paragraph prompted text samples [RWC+19].
paras.zh: 语言为人工智能研究提供了一个天然的领域，因为绝大多数推理任务都可以通过语言高效地表达和评估，而世界上的文本为通过生成式建模进行无监督学习提供了丰富的数据。深度学习在语言建模方面最近取得了快速进展，最先进的模型 [RNSS18, DCLT18, YDY+19, LOG+19, RSR+19] 在许多特定任务上接近人类水平的表现 [WPN+19]，包括生成连贯的多段落提示文本样本 [RWC+19]。

paras.en: One might expect language modeling performance to depend on model architecture, the size of neural models, the computing power used to train them, and the data available for this training process. In this work we will empirically investigate the dependence of language modeling loss on all of these factors, focusing on the Transformer architecture [VSP+17, LSP+18]. The high ceiling and low floor for performance on language tasks allows us to study trends over more than seven orders of magnitude in scale.
paras.zh: 人们可能期望语言建模性能取决于模型架构、神经模型的规模、用于训练它们的算力以及训练过程中可用的数据。在这项工作中，我们将实证研究语言建模损失对所有上述因素的依赖关系，重点关注 Transformer 架构 [VSP+17, LSP+18]。语言任务性能的高上限和低下限使我们能够研究跨越七个数量级以上规模的变化趋势。

paras.en: Throughout we will observe precise power-law scalings for performance as a function of training time, context length, dataset size, model size, and compute budget.
paras.zh: 在整个过程中，我们将观察到性能作为训练时间、上下文长度、数据集大小、模型大小和算力预算的函数所呈现的精确幂律缩放关系。

paras.en: Our key findings for Transformer language models are are as follows:
paras.zh: 我们对Transformer语言模型的主要发现如下：

paras.en: Model performance depends most strongly on scale, which consists of three factors: the number of model parameters N𝑁N (excluding embeddings), the size of the dataset D𝐷D, and the amount of compute C𝐶C used for training. Within reasonable limits, performance depends very weakly on other architectural hyperparameters such as depth vs. width. (Section 3)
paras.zh: 模型性能最强烈地依赖于规模，规模包括三个因素：模型参数数量N（不包括嵌入）、数据集大小D以及用于训练的算力C。在合理范围内，性能对其他架构超参数（如深度与宽度）的依赖性非常弱。（第3节）

paras.en: Performance has a power-law relationship with each of the three scale factors N,D,C𝑁𝐷𝐶N,D,C when not bottlenecked by the other two, with trends spanning more than six orders of magnitude (see Figure 1). We observe no signs of deviation from these trends on the upper end, though performance must flatten out eventually before reaching zero loss. (Section 3)
paras.zh: 当不受其他两个因素瓶颈限制时，性能与三个规模因素N、D、C中的每一个都存在幂律关系，趋势跨越六个数量级以上（见图1）。我们未观察到上端偏离这些趋势的迹象，尽管性能最终必须在达到零损失之前趋于平缓。（第3节）

paras.en: Performance improves predictably as long as we scale up N𝑁N and D𝐷D in tandem, but enters a regime of diminishing returns if either N𝑁N or D𝐷D is held fixed while the other increases. The performance penalty depends predictably on the ratio N0.74/Dsuperscript𝑁0.74𝐷N^{0.74}/D, meaning that every time we increase the model size 8x, we only need to increase the data by roughly 5x to avoid a penalty. (Section 4)
paras.zh: 只要我们同步扩展N和D，性能就会可预测地提升，但如果N或D中一个固定不变而另一个增加，则会进入收益递减的区域。性能惩罚可预测地取决于比率N^0.74/D，这意味着每次我们将模型大小增加8倍时，只需将数据增加大约5倍即可避免惩罚。（第4节）

paras.en: Training curves follow predictable power-laws whose parameters are roughly independent of the model size. By extrapolating the early part of a training curve, we can roughly predict the loss that would be achieved if we trained for much longer. (Section 5)
paras.zh: 训练曲线遵循可预测的幂律，其参数大致与模型大小无关。通过外推训练曲线的早期部分，我们可以大致预测如果训练更长时间会达到的损失。（第5节）

paras.en: When we evaluate models on text with a different distribution than they were trained on, the results are strongly correlated to those on the training validation set with a roughly constant offset in the loss – in other words, transfer to a different distribution incurs a constant penalty but otherwise improves roughly in line with performance on the training set. (Section 3.2.2)
paras.zh: 当我们在与训练数据分布不同的文本上评估模型时，结果与训练验证集上的结果强相关，损失大致有一个常数偏移——换句话说，迁移到不同分布会带来一个常数惩罚，但除此之外，性能大致与训练集上的表现同步提升。（第3.2.2节）

paras.en: Large models are more sample-efficient than small models, reaching the same level of performance with fewer optimization steps (Figure 2) and using fewer data points (Figure 4).
paras.zh: 大模型比小模型更具样本效率，能以更少的优化步骤（图2）和更少的数据点（图4）达到相同的性能水平。

paras.en: When working within a fixed compute budget C𝐶C but without any other restrictions on the model size N𝑁N or available data D𝐷D, we attain optimal performance by training very large models and stopping significantly short of convergence (see Figure 3). Maximally compute-efficient training would therefore be far more sample efficient than one might expect based on training small models to convergence, with data requirements growing very slowly as D∼C0.27similar-to𝐷superscript𝐶0.27D\sim C^{0.27} with training compute. (Section 6)
paras.zh: 在固定的算力预算C下，但对模型大小N或可用数据D没有其他限制时，我们通过训练非常大的模型并在远未收敛时停止来获得最优性能（见图3）。因此，最大化算力效率的训练将比基于训练小模型至收敛所预期的样本效率高得多，数据需求随训练算力增长非常缓慢，D ~ C^0.27。（第6节）

paras.en: The ideal batch size for training these models is roughly a power of the loss only, and continues to be determinable by measuring the gradient noise scale [MKAT18]; it is roughly 1-2 million tokens at convergence for the largest models we can train. (Section 5.1)
paras.zh: 训练这些模型的理想批量大小大致是损失的一个幂函数，并且仍然可以通过测量梯度噪声尺度[MKAT18]来确定；对于我们能够训练的最大模型，在收敛时大约为1-2百万个词元。（第5.1节）

paras.en: Taken together, these results show that language modeling performance improves smoothly and predictably as we appropriately scale up model size, data, and compute. We expect that larger language models will perform better and be more sample efficient than current models.
paras.zh: 综合来看，这些结果表明，随着我们适当扩展模型大小、数据和算力，语言建模性能会平滑且可预测地提升。我们预期更大的语言模型将比当前模型表现更好且更具样本效率。

paras.en: These relations hold across eight orders of magnitude in Cminsubscript𝐶minC_{\rm min}, six orders of magnitude in N𝑁N, and over two orders of magnitude in D𝐷D. They depend very weakly on model shape and other Transformer hyperparameters (depth, width, number of self-attention heads), with specific numerical values associated with the Webtext2 training set [RWC+19]. The power laws αN,αD,αCminsubscript𝛼Nsubscript𝛼Dsuperscriptsubscript𝛼𝐶min\alpha_{\rm N},\alpha_{\rm D},\alpha_{C}^{\rm min} specify the degree of performance improvement expected as we scale up N𝑁N, D𝐷D, or Cminsubscript𝐶minC_{\rm min}; for example, doubling the number of parameters yields a loss that is smaller by a factor 2−αN=0.95superscript2subscript𝛼𝑁0.952^{-\alpha_{N}}=0.95. The precise numerical values of Nc,Ccmin,subscript𝑁csuperscriptsubscript𝐶cminN_{\mathrm{c}},C_{\rm c}^{\rm min}, and Dcsubscript𝐷cD_{\mathrm{c}} depend on the vocabulary size and tokenization and hence do not have a fundamental meaning.
paras.zh: 这些关系在 C_min 的八个数量级、N 的六个数量级和 D 的两个数量级上成立。它们对模型形状和其他 Transformer 超参数（深度、宽度、自注意力头数）的依赖非常弱，具体数值与 Webtext2 训练集相关 [RWC+19]。幂律指数 α_N、α_D、α_C^min 指定了随着 N、D 或 C_min 扩大时预期的性能提升程度；例如，参数数量翻倍会使损失减少因子 2^{-α_N}=0.95。N_c、C_c^min 和 D_c 的精确数值取决于词汇表大小和分词方式，因此没有根本性意义。

paras.zh: 决定数据并行速度/效率权衡的临界批量大小 [MKAT18] 也大致服从关于 L 的幂律：

In fact, we find that there is a single equation combining (1.1) and (1.2) that governs the simultaneous dependence on N𝑁N and D𝐷D and governs the degree of overfitting:

paras.zh: 当在固定算力预算 C 下训练且无其他约束时，方程 (1.6) 预测最优模型大小 N、最优批量大小 B、最优步数 S 和数据集大小 D 应增长为：

paras.en: which closely matches the empirically optimal results N∝Cmin0.73proportional-to𝑁superscriptsubscript𝐶min0.73N\propto C_{\rm min}^{0.73}, B∝Cmin0.24proportional-to𝐵superscriptsubscript𝐶min0.24B\propto C_{\rm min}^{0.24}, and S∝Cmin0.03proportional-to𝑆superscriptsubscript𝐶min0.03S\propto C_{\rm min}^{0.03}. As the computational budget C𝐶C increases, it should be spent primarily on larger models, without dramatic increases in training time or dataset size (see Figure 3). This also implies that as models grow larger, they become increasingly sample efficient. In practice, researchers typically train smaller models for longer than would be maximally compute-efficient because of hardware constraints. Optimal performance depends on total compute as a power law (see Equation (1.3)).
paras.zh: 这与经验最优结果 N ∝ C_min^{0.73}、B ∝ C_min^{0.24} 和 S ∝ C_min^{0.03} 非常吻合。随着计算预算 C 增加，应主要花费在更大的模型上，而训练时间或数据集大小不会显著增加（见图 3）。这也意味着随着模型变大，它们变得越来越样本高效。在实践中，由于硬件限制，研究人员通常训练较小的模型时间更长，而不是最大程度地实现算力高效。最优性能取决于总算力，呈幂律关系（见方程 (1.3)）。

paras.en: We provide some basic theoretical motivation for Equation (1.5), an analysis of learning curve fits and their implications for training time, and a breakdown of our results per token. We also make some brief comparisons to LSTMs and recurrent Transformers [DGV+18].
paras.zh: 我们为方程 (1.5) 提供了一些基本的理论动机，分析了学习曲线拟合及其对训练时间的影响，并给出了每个 token 的结果分解。我们还与 LSTM 和循环 Transformer [DGV+18] 进行了简要比较。

paras.en: L𝐿L – the cross entropy loss in nats. Typically it will be averaged over the tokens in a context, but in some cases we report the loss for specific tokens within the context.
paras.zh: L𝐿L – 以 nats 为单位的交叉熵损失。通常它会在上下文中的词元上取平均，但在某些情况下我们会报告上下文中特定词元的损失。

paras.en: N𝑁N – the number of model parameters, excluding all vocabulary and positional embeddings
paras.zh: N𝑁N – 模型参数的数量，不包括所有词汇嵌入和位置嵌入。

paras.en: C≈6​N​B​S𝐶6𝑁𝐵𝑆C\approx 6NBS – an estimate of the total non-embedding training compute, where B𝐵B is the batch size, and S𝑆S is the number of training steps (ie parameter updates). We quote numerical values in PF-days, where one PF-day =1015×24×3600=8.64×1019absentsuperscript10152436008.64superscript1019=10^{15}\times 24\times 3600=8.64\times 10^{19} floating point operations.
paras.zh: C≈6NBS𝐶6𝑁𝐵𝑆C\approx 6NBS – 非嵌入训练算力的总估计，其中 B𝐵B 是批量大小，S𝑆S 是训练步数（即参数更新次数）。我们以 PF-天为单位给出数值，其中 1 PF-天 =10^15×24×3600=8.64×10^19 次浮点运算。

paras.en: Bcritsubscript𝐵critB_{\rm crit} – the critical batch size [MKAT18], defined and discussed in Section 5.1. Training at the critical batch size provides a roughly optimal compromise between time and compute efficiency.
paras.zh: Bcrit𝐵critB_{\rm crit} – 临界批量大小 [MKAT18]，在第 5.1 节中定义和讨论。以临界批量大小进行训练可在时间和算力效率之间实现大致最优的折衷。

paras.en: Cminsubscript𝐶minC_{\rm min} – an estimate of the minimum amount of non-embedding compute to reach a given value of the loss. This is the training compute that would be used if the model were trained at a batch size much less than the critical batch size.
paras.zh: Cmin𝐶minC_{\rm min} – 达到给定损失值所需的最小非嵌入算力估计。这是如果模型以远小于临界批量大小的批量大小进行训练时将使用的训练算力。

paras.en: Sminsubscript𝑆minS_{\rm min} – an estimate of the minimal number of training steps needed to reach a given value of the loss. This is also the number of training steps that would be used if the model were trained at a batch size much greater than the critical batch size.
paras.zh: Smin𝑆minS_{\rm min} – 达到给定损失值所需的最小训练步数估计。这也是如果模型以远大于临界批量大小的批量大小进行训练时将使用的训练步数。

paras.en: αXsubscript𝛼𝑋\alpha_{X} – power-law exponents for the scaling of the loss as L​(X)∝1/XαXproportional-to𝐿𝑋1superscript𝑋subscript𝛼𝑋L(X)\propto 1/X^{\alpha_{X}} where X𝑋X can be any of N,D,C,S,B,Cmin𝑁𝐷𝐶𝑆𝐵superscript𝐶minN,D,C,S,B,C^{\rm min}.
paras.zh: αX𝛼X – 损失缩放关系的幂律指数，满足 L(X)∝1/X^αX，其中 X 可以是 N、D、C、S、B、C^min 中的任意一个。

paras.en: We train language models on WebText2, an extended version of the WebText [RWC+19] dataset, tokenized using byte-pair encoding [SHB15] with a vocabulary size nvocab=50257subscript𝑛vocab50257n_{\rm vocab}=50257. We optimize the autoregressive log-likelihood (i.e. cross-entropy loss) averaged over a 1024-token context, which is also our principal performance metric. We record the loss on the WebText2 test distribution and on a selection of other text distributions. We primarily train decoder-only [LSP+18, RNSS18] Transformer [VSP+17] models, though we also train LSTM models and Universal Transformers [DGV+18] for comparison.
paras.zh: 我们在WebText2上训练语言模型，WebText2是WebText [RWC+19]数据集的扩展版本，使用字节对编码 [SHB15]进行分词，词汇量大小为nvocab=50257。我们优化自回归对数似然（即交叉熵损失），在1024个token的上下文窗口上取平均，这也是我们的主要性能指标。我们在WebText2测试分布以及其他一些文本分布上记录损失。我们主要训练仅解码器 [LSP+18, RNSS18] Transformer [VSP+17]模型，但也训练LSTM模型和Universal Transformers [DGV+18]以进行比较。

full.sec: 2.1 Parameter and Compute Scaling of Transformers

paras.en: We parameterize the Transformer architecture using hyperparameters nlayersubscript𝑛layern_{\rm layer} (number of layers), dmodelsubscript𝑑modeld_{{\rm model}} (dimension of the residual stream), dffsubscript𝑑ffd_{\rm ff} (dimension of the intermediate feed-forward layer), dattnsubscript𝑑attnd_{\rm attn} (dimension of the attention output), and nheadssubscript𝑛headsn_{\rm heads} (number of attention heads per layer). We include nctxsubscript𝑛ctxn_{\rm ctx} tokens in the input context, with nctx=1024subscript𝑛ctx1024n_{\rm ctx}=1024 except where otherwise noted.
paras.zh: 我们使用超参数 n_layer（层数）、d_model（残差流维度）、d_ff（前馈中间层维度）、d_attn（注意力输出维度）和 n_heads（每层注意力头数）来参数化 Transformer 架构。输入上下文包含 n_ctx 个 token，除非另有说明，n_ctx=1024。

paras.en: We use N𝑁N to denote the model size, which we define as the number of non-embedding parameters
paras.zh: 我们用 N 表示模型大小，定义为非嵌入参数的数量

paras.en: where we have excluded biases and other sub-leading terms. Our models also have nvocab​dmodelsubscript𝑛vocabsubscript𝑑modeln_{\rm vocab}d_{{\rm model}} parameters in an embedding matrix, and use nctx​dmodelsubscript𝑛ctxsubscript𝑑modeln_{\rm ctx}d_{{\rm model}} parameters for positional embeddings, but we do not include these when discussing the ‘model size’ N𝑁N; we will see that this produces significantly cleaner scaling laws.
paras.zh: 其中我们排除了偏置项和其他次主导项。我们的模型在嵌入矩阵中还有 n_vocab * d_model 个参数，并使用 n_ctx * d_model 个参数用于位置嵌入，但在讨论“模型大小”N 时我们不包含这些；我们将看到这会产生更清晰的缩放定律。

paras.en: Evaluating a forward pass of the Transformer involves roughly
paras.zh: 评估 Transformer 的前向传播大致涉及

paras.en: add-multiply operations, where the factor of two comes from the multiply-accumulate operation used in matrix multiplication. A more detailed per-operation parameter and compute count is included in Table 1.
paras.zh: 次加法-乘法运算，其中因子 2 来自矩阵乘法中使用的乘累加操作。更详细的每操作参数和算力计数见表 1。

paras.en: For contexts and models with dmodel>nctx/12subscript𝑑modelsubscript𝑛ctx12d_{{\rm model}}>n_{\rm ctx}/12, the context-dependent computational cost per token is a relatively small fraction of the total compute. Since we primarily study models where dmodel≫nctx/12much-greater-thansubscript𝑑modelsubscript𝑛ctx12d_{\rm model}\gg n_{\rm ctx}/12, we do not include context-dependent terms in our training compute estimate. Accounting for the backwards pass (approximately twice the compute as the forwards pass), we then define the estimated non-embedding compute as C≈6​N𝐶6𝑁C\approx 6N floating point operators per training token.
paras.zh: 对于 d_model > n_ctx/12 的上下文和模型，每个 token 的上下文相关计算成本占总算力的比例相对较小。由于我们主要研究 d_model ≫ n_ctx/12 的模型，因此在训练算力估计中不包含上下文相关项。考虑反向传播（大约为前向传播算力的两倍），我们定义每个训练 token 的估计非嵌入算力为 C ≈ 6N 次浮点运算。

paras.en: Unless otherwise noted, we train models with the Adam optimizer [KB14] for a fixed 2.5×1052.5superscript1052.5\times 10^{5} steps with a batch size of 512512512 sequences of 102410241024 tokens. Due to memory constraints, our largest models (more than 1B parameters) were trained with Adafactor [SS18]. We experimented with a variety of learning rates and schedules, as discussed in Appendix D.6. We found that results at convergence were largely independent of learning rate schedule. Unless otherwise noted, all training runs included in our data used a learning rate schedule with a 3000 step linear warmup followed by a cosine decay to zero.
paras.zh: 除非另有说明，我们使用 Adam 优化器 [KB14] 训练模型，固定进行 2.5×10^5 步，批大小为 512 个序列，每个序列 1024 个 token。由于内存限制，我们最大的模型（超过 10 亿参数）使用 Adafactor [SS18] 进行训练。我们尝试了多种学习率和调度策略，详见附录 D.6。我们发现收敛时的结果在很大程度上与学习率调度无关。除非另有说明，我们数据中包含的所有训练运行均使用具有 3000 步线性预热然后余弦衰减至零的学习率调度。

paras.en: We train our models on an extended version of the WebText dataset described in [RWC+19]. The original WebText dataset was a web scrape of outbound links from Reddit through December 2017 which received at least 3 karma. In the second version, WebText2, we added outbound Reddit links from the period of January to October 2018, also with a minimum of 3 karma. The karma threshold served as a heuristic for whether people found the link interesting or useful. The text of the new links was extracted with the Newspaper3k python library. In total, the dataset consists of 20.3M documents containing 96 GB of text and 1.62×10101.62superscript10101.62\times 10^{10} words (as defined by wc). We then apply the reversible tokenizer described in [RWC+19], which yields 2.29×10102.29superscript10102.29\times 10^{10} tokens. We reserve 6.6×1086.6superscript1086.6\times 10^{8} of these tokens for use as a test set, and we also test on similarly-prepared samples of Books Corpus [ZKZ+15], Common Crawl [Fou], English Wikipedia, and a collection of publicly-available Internet Books.
paras.zh: 我们在 [RWC+19] 中描述的 WebText 数据集的扩展版本上训练模型。原始 WebText 数据集是对截至 2017 年 12 月来自 Reddit 的外链进行网络抓取，这些链接至少获得了 3 个 karma。在第二个版本 WebText2 中，我们添加了 2018 年 1 月至 10 月期间来自 Reddit 的外链，同样要求至少 3 个 karma。karma 阈值作为一种启发式方法，用于判断人们是否认为该链接有趣或有用。新链接的文本使用 Newspaper3k Python 库提取。总体而言，该数据集包含 2030 万个文档，共计 96 GB 文本和 1.62×10^10 个单词（按 wc 定义）。然后，我们应用 [RWC+19] 中描述的可逆分词器，得到 2.29×10^10 个 token。我们保留其中 6.6×10^8 个 token 作为测试集，并在同样预处理过的 Books Corpus [ZKZ+15]、Common Crawl [Fou]、英文维基百科以及一个公开可用的互联网图书集合样本上进行测试。

full.sec: 3 Empirical Results and Basic Power Laws

paras.en: To characterize language model scaling we train a wide variety of models, varying a number of factors including:
paras.zh: 为了刻画语言模型缩放的特征，我们训练了多种模型，并变化了多个因素，包括：

paras.en: Model size (ranging in size from 768 to 1.5 billion non-embedding parameters)
paras.zh: 模型大小（范围从 768 到 15 亿非嵌入参数）

paras.en: Dataset size (ranging from 22 million to 23 billion tokens)
paras.zh: 数据集大小（范围从 2200 万到 230 亿词元）

paras.en: Shape (including depth, width, attention heads, and feed-forward dimension)
paras.zh: 形状（包括深度、宽度、注意力头数和前馈维度）

paras.en: Context length (1024 for most runs, though we also experiment with shorter contexts)
paras.zh: 上下文长度（大多数运行使用 1024，但我们也尝试了更短的上下文）

paras.en: Batch size (219superscript2192^{19} for most runs, but we also vary it to measure the critical batch size)
paras.zh: 批量大小（大多数运行使用 2^19，但我们也改变它以测量临界批量大小）

paras.en: In this section we will display data along with empirically-motivated fits, deferring theoretical analysis to later sections.
paras.zh: 在本节中，我们将展示数据以及基于经验拟合的结果，将理论分析留到后续章节。

full.sec: 3.1 Approximate Transformer Shape and Hyperparameter Independence
full.secZh: 3.1 近似Transformer形状与超参数独立性

paras.en: Transformer performance depends very weakly on the shape parameters nlayer,nheadssubscript𝑛layersubscript𝑛headsn_{\rm layer},n_{\rm heads}, and dffsubscript𝑑ffd_{\rm ff} when we hold the total non-embedding parameter count N𝑁N fixed. To establish these results we trained models with fixed size while varying a single hyperparameter. This was simplest for the case of nheadssubscript𝑛headsn_{\rm heads}. When varying nlayersubscript𝑛layern_{\rm layer}, we simultaneously varied dmodelsubscript𝑑modeld_{{\rm model}} while keeping N≈12​nlayer​dmodel2𝑁12subscript𝑛layersuperscriptsubscript𝑑model2N\approx 12n_{\rm layer}d_{{\rm model}}^{2} fixed. Similarly, to vary dffsubscript𝑑ffd_{\rm ff} at fixed model size we also simultaneously varied the dmodelsubscript𝑑modeld_{{\rm model}} parameter, as required by the parameter counts in Table 1. Independence of nlayerssubscript𝑛layersn_{\rm layers} would follow if deeper Transformers effectively behave as ensembles of shallower models, as has been suggested for ResNets [VWB16]. The results are shown in Figure 5.
paras.zh: 当固定非嵌入参数总数N时，Transformer的性能对形状参数n_layer、n_heads和d_ff的依赖非常弱。为确立这些结果，我们训练了固定大小但改变单个超参数的模型。对于n_heads的情况，这最为简单。当改变n_layer时，我们同时改变d_model，同时保持N≈12n_layer d_model²固定。类似地，为了在固定模型大小下改变d_ff，我们也同时改变d_model参数，如表1中的参数计数所示。n_layers的独立性如果成立，则意味着更深的Transformer实际上表现为浅层模型的集成，正如ResNets所提出的[VWB16]。结果如图5所示。

full.sec: 3.2 Performance with Non-Embedding Parameter Count N𝑁N
full.secZh: 3.2 非嵌入参数计数 N 下的性能

paras.en: In Figure 6 we display the performance of a wide variety of models, ranging from small models with shape (nlayer,dmodel)=(2,128)subscript𝑛layersubscript𝑑model2128(n_{\rm layer},d_{{\rm model}})=(2,128) through billion-parameter models, ranging in shape from (6,4288)64288(6,4288) through (207,768)207768(207,768). Here we have trained to near convergence on the full WebText2 dataset and observe no overfitting (except possibly for the very largest models).
paras.zh: 在图6中，我们展示了各种模型的性能，从形状为 (n_layer, d_model) = (2, 128) 的小模型到十亿参数级别的模型，形状范围从 (6, 4288) 到 (207, 768)。这里我们在完整的 WebText2 数据集上训练至接近收敛，并且没有观察到过拟合（除了可能最大的模型）。

paras.en: As shown in Figure 1, we find a steady trend with non-embedding parameter count N𝑁N, which can be fit to the first term of Equation (1.5), so that
paras.zh: 如图1所示，我们发现随着非嵌入参数计数 N 的稳定趋势，可以拟合到方程 (1.5) 的第一项，因此

paras.en: To observe these trends it is crucial to study performance as a function of N𝑁N; if we instead use the total parameter count (including the embedding parameters) the trend is somewhat obscured (see Figure 6). This suggests that the embedding matrix can be made smaller without impacting performance, as has been seen in recent work [LCG+19].
paras.zh: 为了观察这些趋势，研究性能作为 N 的函数至关重要；如果我们改用总参数计数（包括嵌入参数），趋势会有些模糊（见图6）。这表明嵌入矩阵可以做得更小而不影响性能，正如最近的工作 [LCG+19] 中所见。

paras.en: Although these models have been trained on the WebText2 dataset, their test loss on a variety of other datasets is also a power-law in N𝑁N with nearly identical power, as shown in Figure 8.
paras.zh: 尽管这些模型是在 WebText2 数据集上训练的，但它们在各种其他数据集上的测试损失也是 N 的幂律，且幂次几乎相同，如图8所示。

paras.en: In Figure 7 we compare LSTM and Transformer performance as a function of non-embedding parameter count N𝑁N. The LSTMs were trained with the same dataset and context length. We see from these figures that the LSTMs perform as well as Transformers for tokens appearing early in the context, but cannot match the Transformer performance for later tokens. We present power-law relationships between performance and context position Appendix D.5, where increasingly large powers for larger models suggest improved ability to quickly recognize patterns.
paras.zh: 在图7中，我们比较了 LSTM 和 Transformer 作为非嵌入参数计数 N 的函数的性能。LSTM 使用相同的数据集和上下文长度进行训练。从这些图中我们看到，对于出现在上下文早期的词元，LSTM 的表现与 Transformer 相当，但无法匹配 Transformer 对后期词元的性能。我们在附录 D.5 中给出了性能与上下文位置之间的幂律关系，其中较大模型的幂次越来越大，表明其快速识别模式的能力有所提高。

paras.en: We also compare the performance of standard Transformers to recurrent Transformers [DGV+18] in Figure 17 in the appendix. These models re-use parameters, and so perform slightly better as a function of N𝑁N, at the cost of additional compute per-parameter.
paras.zh: 我们还在附录的图17中比较了标准 Transformer 与循环 Transformer [DGV+18] 的性能。这些模型重用参数，因此作为 N 的函数性能略好，但代价是每个参数需要额外的算力。

paras.en: We have also tested our models on a set of additional text data distributions. The test loss on these datasets as a function of model size is shown in Figure 8; in all cases the models were trained only on the WebText2 dataset. We see that the loss on these other data distributions improves smoothly with model size, in direct parallel with the improvement on WebText2. We find that generalization depends almost exclusively on the in-distribution validation loss, and does not depend on the duration of training or proximity to convergence. We also observe no dependence on model depth (see Appendix D.8).
paras.zh: 我们还在一组额外的文本数据分布上测试了我们的模型。这些数据集上的测试损失随模型大小的变化如图8所示；在所有情况下，模型仅在 WebText2 数据集上训练。我们看到，这些其他数据分布上的损失随模型大小平滑改善，与 WebText2 上的改善直接平行。我们发现泛化几乎完全取决于分布内验证损失，而不依赖于训练时长或接近收敛的程度。我们也没有观察到对模型深度的依赖（见附录 D.8）。

We stopped training once the test loss ceased to decrease. We see that the resulting test losses can be fit with simple power-law

The data strongly suggests that sample efficiency improves with model size, and we also illustrate this directly in Figure 19 in the appendix.

full.sec: 4 Charting the Infinite Data Limit and Overfitting
full.secZh: 4 描绘无限数据极限与过拟合

paras.en: In Section 3 we found a number of basic scaling laws for language modeling performance. Here we will study the performance of a model of size N𝑁N trained on a dataset with D𝐷D tokens while varying N𝑁N and D𝐷D simultaneously. We will empirically demonstrate that the optimally trained test loss accords with the scaling law of Equation (1.5). This provides guidance on how much data we would need to train models of increasing size while keeping overfitting under control.
paras.zh: 在第3节中，我们发现了语言建模性能的一些基本缩放定律。这里我们将研究一个大小为N的模型在包含D个token的数据集上训练时的性能，同时改变N和D。我们将通过实验证明，最优训练下的测试损失符合公式(1.5)的缩放定律。这为我们提供了指导，说明在控制过拟合的同时，训练规模不断增大的模型需要多少数据。

paras.en: We have chosen the parameterization (1.5) (repeated here for convenience):
paras.zh: 我们选择了参数化形式 (1.5)（为方便起见在此重复）：

paras.en: Changes in vocabulary size or tokenization are expected to rescale the loss by an overall factor. The parameterization of L​(N,D)𝐿𝑁𝐷L(N,D) (and all models of the loss) must naturally allow for such a rescaling.
paras.zh: 词汇量或分词方式的变化预计会通过一个整体因子重新缩放损失。L(N,D) 的参数化（以及所有损失模型）必须自然地允许这种重新缩放。

paras.en: Fixing D𝐷D and sending N→∞→𝑁N\to\infty, the overall loss should approach L​(D)𝐿𝐷L(D). Conversely, fixing N𝑁N and sending D→∞→𝐷D\to\infty the loss must approach L​(N)𝐿𝑁L(N).
paras.zh: 固定 D 并令 N→∞，整体损失应趋近于 L(D)。反之，固定 N 并令 D→∞，损失必须趋近于 L(N)。

paras.en: L​(N,D)𝐿𝑁𝐷L(N,D) should be analytic at D=∞𝐷D=\infty, so that it has a series expansion in 1/D1𝐷1/D with integer powers. Theoretical support for this principle is significantly weaker than for the first two.
paras.zh: L(N,D) 应在 D=∞ 处解析，从而具有 1/D 的整数幂级数展开。这一原则的理论支持比前两个要弱得多。

paras.en: Our choice of L​(N,D)𝐿𝑁𝐷L(N,D) satisfies the first requirement because we can rescale Nc,Dcsubscript𝑁𝑐subscript𝐷𝑐N_{c},D_{c} with changes in the vocabulary. This also implies that the values of Nc,Dcsubscript𝑁𝑐subscript𝐷𝑐N_{c},D_{c} have no fundamental meaning.
paras.zh: 我们选择的 L(N,D) 满足第一个要求，因为我们可以通过重新缩放 Nc, Dc 来适应词汇量的变化。这也意味着 Nc, Dc 的值没有根本意义。

paras.en: Since we stop training early when the test loss ceases to improve and optimize all models in the same way, we expect that larger models should always perform better than smaller models. But with fixed finite D𝐷D, we also do not expect any model to be capable of approaching the best possible loss (ie the entropy of text). Similarly, a model with fixed size will be capacity-limited. These considerations motivate our second principle. Note that knowledge of L​(N)𝐿𝑁L(N) at infinite D𝐷D and L​(D)𝐿𝐷L(D) at infinite N𝑁N fully determines all the parameters in L​(N,D)𝐿𝑁𝐷L(N,D).
paras.zh: 由于我们在测试损失停止改善时提前停止训练，并以相同方式优化所有模型，我们预期更大的模型应始终优于更小的模型。但在固定有限 D 的情况下，我们也不期望任何模型能够接近最佳可能损失（即文本的熵）。类似地，固定大小的模型将受限于容量。这些考虑促使我们提出第二个原则。注意，在无限 D 下的 L(N) 和无限 N 下的 L(D) 完全确定了 L(N,D) 中的所有参数。

paras.en: The third principle is more speculative. There is a simple and general reason one might expect overfitting to scale ∝1/Dproportional-toabsent1𝐷\propto 1/D at very large D𝐷D. Overfitting should be related to the variance or the signal-to-noise ratio of the dataset [AS17], and this scales as 1/D1𝐷1/D. This expectation should hold for any smooth loss function, since we expect to be able to expand the loss about the D→∞→𝐷D\to\infty limit. However, this argument assumes that 1/D1𝐷1/D corrections dominate over other sources of variance, such as the finite batch size and other limits on the efficacy of optimization. Without empirical confirmation, we would not be very confident of its applicability.
paras.zh: 第三个原则更具推测性。有一个简单而普遍的原因可以预期过拟合在非常大的 D 下与 1/D 成比例。过拟合应与数据集的方差或信噪比有关 [AS17]，而这与 1/D 成比例。这一预期应适用于任何光滑损失函数，因为我们期望能够将损失在 D→∞ 极限附近展开。然而，这一论证假设 1/D 修正项主导了其他方差来源，例如有限批次大小和优化效率的其他限制。没有经验确认，我们对其适用性不太有信心。

paras.en: Our third principle explains the asymmetry between the roles of N𝑁N and D𝐷D in Equation (1.5). Very similar symmetric expressions333For example, one might have used L​(N,D)=[(NcN)αN+(DcD)αD]β𝐿𝑁𝐷superscriptdelimited-[]superscriptsubscript𝑁𝑐𝑁subscript𝛼𝑁superscriptsubscript𝐷𝑐𝐷subscript𝛼𝐷𝛽L(N,D)=\left[\left(\frac{N_{c}}{N}\right)^{\alpha_{N}}+\left(\frac{D_{c}}{D}\right)^{\alpha_{D}}\right]^{\beta}, but this does not have a 1/D1𝐷1/D expansion. are possible, but they would not have a 1/D1𝐷1/D expansion with integer powers, and would require the introduction of an additional parameter.
paras.zh: 我们的第三个原则解释了方程 (1.5) 中 N 和 D 作用的不对称性。非常相似的对称表达式333例如，可能使用 L(N,D)=[(Nc/N)^αN + (Dc/D)^αD]^β，但这不具有 1/D 展开。是可能的，但它们不会有整数幂的 1/D 展开，并且需要引入额外参数。

paras.en: In any case, we will see that our equation for L​(N,D)𝐿𝑁𝐷L(N,D) fits the data well, which is the most important justification for our L​(N,D)𝐿𝑁𝐷L(N,D) ansatz.
paras.zh: 无论如何，我们将看到我们的 L(N,D) 方程很好地拟合了数据，这是对我们 L(N,D) 假设最重要的证明。

paras.en: We regularize all our models with 10% dropout, and by tracking test loss and stopping once it is no longer decreasing. The results are displayed in Figure 9, including a fit to the four parameters αN,αD,Nc,Dcsubscript𝛼𝑁subscript𝛼𝐷subscript𝑁𝑐subscript𝐷𝑐\alpha_{N},\alpha_{D},N_{c},D_{c} in Equation (1.5):
paras.zh: 我们通过使用10%的dropout以及跟踪测试损失并在其不再下降时停止训练来正则化所有模型。结果如图9所示，包括对公式(1.5)中四个参数αN, αD, Nc, Dc的拟合：

paras.en: We obtain an excellent fit, with the exception of the runs where the dataset has been reduced by a factor of 102410241024, to about 2×1072superscript1072\times 10^{7} tokens. With such a small dataset, an epoch consists of only 40 parameter updates. Perhaps such a tiny dataset represents a different regime for language modeling, as overfitting happens very early in training (see Figure 16). Also note that the parameters differ very slightly from those obtained in Section 3, as here we are fitting the full L​(N,D)𝐿𝑁𝐷L(N,D) rather than just L​(N,∞)𝐿𝑁L(N,\infty) or L​(∞,D)𝐿𝐷L(\infty,D).
paras.zh: 我们获得了极好的拟合，除了数据集被缩小1024倍至约2×10^7个token的实验。对于如此小的数据集，一个epoch仅包含40次参数更新。也许这样小的数据集代表了语言建模的不同机制，因为过拟合在训练早期就发生了（见图16）。另请注意，参数与第3节中获得的略有不同，因为这里我们拟合的是完整的L(N,D)，而不仅仅是L(N,∞)或L(∞,D)。

paras.en: With this relation, models smaller than 109superscript10910^{9} parameters can be trained with minimal overfitting on the 22B token WebText2 dataset, but our largest models will encounter some mild overfitting. More generally, this relation shows that dataset size may grow sub-linearly in model size while avoiding overfitting. Note however that this does not typically represent maximally compute-efficient training. We should also emphasize that we have not optimized regularization (eg the dropout probability) while varying dataset and model size.

full.sec: 5 Scaling Laws with Model Size and Training Time
full.secZh: 5 模型大小与训练时间的缩放定律

paras.en: In this section we will demonstrate that a simple scaling law provides a good description for the loss as a function of model size N𝑁N and training time. First we will explain how to use the results of [MKAT18] to define a universal training step Sminsubscript𝑆minS_{\rm min}, which accounts for the fact that most of our models have not been trained at an optimal batch size. Then we will demonstrate that we can fit the model size and training time dependence of the loss using Equation (1.6). Later we will use these results to predict the optimal allocation of training compute between model size and training time, and then confirm that prediction.
paras.zh: 在本节中，我们将证明一个简单的缩放定律能够很好地描述损失作为模型大小 N 和训练时间的函数。首先，我们将解释如何利用 [MKAT18] 的结果来定义一个通用的训练步数 S_min，该步数考虑了大多数模型并非在最优批量大小下训练的事实。然后，我们将证明可以使用方程 (1.6) 来拟合损失对模型大小和训练时间的依赖性。随后，我们将利用这些结果来预测训练算力在模型大小和训练时间之间的最优分配，并验证该预测。

full.sec: 5.1 Adjustment for Training at Bcrit​(L)subscript𝐵crit𝐿B_{\rm crit}(L)
full.secZh: 5.1 针对 Bcrit(L) 的训练调整

paras.en: A simple empirical theory for the batch size dependence of training was developed in [MKAT18] (see also [SLA+18, ZLN+19]). It was argued that there is a critical batch size Bcritsubscript𝐵critB_{\rm crit} for training; for B𝐵B up to Bcritsubscript𝐵critB_{\rm crit} the batch size can be increased with very minimal degradation in compute-efficiency, whereas for B>Bcrit𝐵subscript𝐵critB>B_{\rm crit} increases in B𝐵B result in diminishing returns. It was also argued that the gradient noise scale provides a simple prediction for Bcritsubscript𝐵critB_{\rm crit}, and that neither depends directly on model size except through the value of the loss that has been attained. These results can be used to predict how training time and compute will vary with the batch size. To utilize both training time and compute as effectively as possible, it is best to train with a batch size B≈Bcrit𝐵subscript𝐵critB\approx B_{\rm crit}. Training at B≫Bcritmuch-greater-than𝐵subscript𝐵critB\gg B_{\rm crit} minimizes the number of training steps, while B≪Bcritmuch-less-than𝐵subscript𝐵critB\ll B_{\rm crit} minimizes the use of compute.
paras.zh: [MKAT18] 提出了一种关于训练中批次大小依赖性的简单经验理论（另见 [SLA+18, ZLN+19]）。该理论认为存在一个临界批次大小 Bcrit；当 B 不超过 Bcrit 时，增加批次大小对计算效率的损害极小，而当 B > Bcrit 时，增加 B 会导致收益递减。该理论还认为，梯度噪声尺度为 Bcrit 提供了一个简单的预测，且两者都不直接依赖于模型大小，而仅依赖于已达到的损失值。这些结果可用于预测训练时间和计算量如何随批次大小变化。为了尽可能高效地利用训练时间和计算量，最好使用接近 Bcrit 的批次大小进行训练。使用 B >> Bcrit 训练可最小化训练步数，而 B << Bcrit 则最小化计算量。

full.sec: 6 Optimal Allocation of the Compute Budget

paras.en: We displayed the empirical trend of performance as a function of the computation used during training in the top-right of Figure 1. However, this result involved training at a fixed batch size B𝐵B, whereas we know that in fact we could train more efficiently555One might ask why we did not simply train at Bcritsubscript𝐵critB_{\rm crit} in the first place. The reason is that it depends not only on the model but also on the target value of the loss we wish to achieve, and so is a moving target. by training at the batch size Bcritsubscript𝐵critB_{\rm crit} discussed in Section 5.1. Large and small values of the loss could have been achieved with fewer samples or fewer steps, respectively, and correcting for this inefficiency by standardizing to the critical batch size results in cleaner and more predictable trends.
paras.zh: 我们在图1右上角展示了性能随训练所用计算量变化的经验趋势。然而，这一结果是在固定批次大小 B 下训练的，而实际上我们可以通过使用第5.1节讨论的临界批次大小 B_crit 来更高效地训练555有人可能会问，为什么我们不一开始就直接用 B_crit 训练。原因在于，它不仅取决于模型，还取决于我们想要达到的目标损失值，因此是一个移动目标。通过以临界批次大小训练，大损失值和小损失值可以分别用更少的样本或更少的步数实现，而通过标准化到临界批次大小来纠正这种低效率，可以得到更清晰、更可预测的趋势。

paras.en: In this section we will adjust for this oversight. More importantly, we will use the results of Section 5 to determine the optimal allocation of compute between model size N𝑁N and the quantity of data processed during training, namely 2​Bcrit​Smin2subscript𝐵critsubscript𝑆min2B_{\rm crit}S_{\rm min}. We will determine this allocation both empirically and theoretically, by using the equation for L​(N,Smin)𝐿𝑁subscript𝑆minL(N,S_{\rm min}), and we will demonstrate that these methods agree.
paras.zh: 在本节中，我们将修正这一疏忽。更重要的是，我们将利用第5节的结果来确定算力在模型大小 N 和训练期间处理的数据量（即 2 B_crit S_min）之间的最优分配。我们将通过使用 L(N, S_min) 的方程，从经验和理论两方面确定这种分配，并证明这些方法是一致的。

paras.en: Let us first study the loss as a function of the optimally allocated compute from Equation (5.5). The result is plotted in Figure 13, along with a power-law fit. We see that as compared to the compute plot of Figure 1, the new fit with Cminsubscript𝐶minC_{\rm min} is somewhat improved.
paras.zh: 让我们首先研究作为公式(5.5)中最优分配算力的函数的损失。结果绘制在图13中，并附有幂律拟合。我们看到，与图1的算力图相比，使用Cmin的新拟合有所改进。

paras.en: Given L​(Cmin)𝐿subscript𝐶minL(C_{\rm min}), it is natural to ask for the optimal model size N​(Cmin)𝑁subscript𝐶minN(C_{\rm min}) that provides the minimal loss with a given quantity of training compute. The optimal model size is shown in Figure 14. We observe that N​(Cmin)𝑁subscript𝐶minN(C_{\rm min}) can be fit very well with a power-law
paras.zh: 给定L(Cmin)，自然要问提供给定训练算力下最小损失的最优模型大小N(Cmin)。最优模型大小如图14所示。我们观察到N(Cmin)可以很好地用幂律拟合。

paras.en: In Figure 12, we show the effect of training models of sub-optimal sizes (see Appendix B.4).
paras.zh: 在图12中，我们展示了训练次优大小模型的效果（见附录B.4）。

paras.en: By definition Cmin≡6​N​Bcrit​Ssubscript𝐶min6𝑁subscript𝐵crit𝑆C_{\rm min}\equiv 6NB_{\rm crit}S, and so we can use N​(Cmin)𝑁subscript𝐶minN(C_{\rm min}) to extract further results. In particular, since prior fits show B∝L−4.8proportional-to𝐵superscript𝐿4.8B\propto L^{-4.8} and L∝Cmin−0.05proportional-to𝐿superscriptsubscript𝐶min0.05L\propto C_{\rm min}^{-0.05}, we can conclude that Bcrit∝Cmin0.24proportional-tosubscript𝐵critsuperscriptsubscript𝐶min0.24B_{\rm crit}\propto C_{\rm min}^{0.24}. This leads us to conclude that the optimal number of steps will only grow very slowly with compute, as
paras.zh: 根据定义Cmin≡6NBcritS，因此我们可以使用N(Cmin)来提取进一步的结果。特别地，由于先前的拟合显示B∝L^{-4.8}且L∝Cmin^{-0.05}，我们可以得出结论Bcrit∝Cmin^{0.24}。这使我们得出结论，最优步数将随算力增长得非常缓慢，如

paras.en: matching the empirical results in Figure 14. In fact the measured exponent is sufficiently small that our results may even be consistent with an exponent of zero.
paras.zh: 与图14中的经验结果相匹配。实际上，测得的指数足够小，以至于我们的结果甚至可能与指数为零一致。

paras.en: Thus we conclude that as we scale up language modeling with an optimal allocation of computation, we should predominantly increase the model size N𝑁N, while simultaneously scaling up the batch size via B∝Bcritproportional-to𝐵subscript𝐵critB\propto B_{\rm crit} with negligible increase in the number of serial steps. Since compute-efficient training uses relatively few optimization steps, additional work on speeding up early training dynamics may be warranted.
paras.zh: 因此我们得出结论，随着我们以最优算力分配来扩展语言建模，我们应该主要增加模型大小N，同时通过B∝Bcrit来扩展批量大小，而串行步数增加可忽略不计。由于计算高效训练使用相对较少的优化步骤，可能需要额外的工作来加速早期训练动态。

paras.en: The results for L​(Cmin)𝐿subscript𝐶minL(C_{\rm min}) and the allocations can be predicted from the L​(N,Smin)𝐿𝑁subscript𝑆minL(N,S_{\rm min}) equation obtained in Section 5. Given our equation for L​(N,Smin)𝐿𝑁subscript𝑆minL(N,S_{\rm min}), we can substitute Smin=Cmin6​N​Bsubscript𝑆minsubscript𝐶min6𝑁𝐵S_{\rm min}=\frac{C_{\rm min}}{6NB} and then find the minimum of the loss as a function of N𝑁N, while fixing the training compute. We carry out this procedure in detail in Appendix B, where we also provide some additional predictions.
paras.zh: 从第5节得到的 L(N,Smin) 方程可以预测 L(Cmin) 的结果以及分配情况。给定我们的 L(N,Smin) 方程，我们可以代入 Smin = Cmin / (6NB)，然后在固定训练算力的条件下，寻找损失作为 N 的函数的极小值。我们在附录 B 中详细执行了这一过程，并提供了其他一些预测。

paras.en: For the loss as a function of training compute, we predict that
paras.zh: 对于损失作为训练算力的函数，我们预测

paras.en: in excellent agreement with the exponent of Figure 13. We also predict that
paras.zh: 与图13的指数非常吻合。我们还预测

paras.en: which also matches the scaling of Figure 14 to within a few percent. Our scaling laws provide a predictive framework for the performance of language modeling.
paras.zh: 这也与图14的缩放关系在百分之几的误差内一致。我们的缩放定律为语言建模的性能提供了一个预测框架。

paras.en: We observe no signs of deviation from straight power-law trends at large values of compute, data, or model size. Our trends must eventually level off, though, since natural language has non-zero entropy.
paras.zh: 我们观察到，在算力、数据或模型规模较大时，没有偏离直线幂律趋势的迹象。不过，由于自然语言具有非零熵，我们的趋势最终必然会趋于平稳。

paras.en: Indeed, the trends for compute-efficient training described in this section already contain an apparent contradiction. At scales several orders of magnitude above those documented here, the performance predicted by the L​(Cmin)𝐿subscript𝐶minL(C_{\rm min}) scaling law decreases below what should be possible given the slow growth in training data with compute. This implies that our scaling laws must break down before this point, but we conjecture that the intersection point has a deeper meaning: it provides an estimate of the point at which Transformer language models reach maximal performance.
paras.zh: 事实上，本节描述的算力高效训练趋势已经包含了一个明显的矛盾。在比这里记录的规模高出几个数量级时，由 L(Cmin) 缩放定律预测的性能下降到了低于根据算力下训练数据缓慢增长所可能达到的水平。这意味着我们的缩放定律在此之前必然失效，但我们猜想这个交点具有更深层的含义：它提供了 Transformer 语言模型达到最大性能点的估计。

paras.en: Since the amount of data used by compute-efficient training grows slowly with the compute budget, the performance predicted by L​(Cmin)𝐿subscript𝐶minL(C_{\rm min}) eventually hits a lower bound set by the L​(D)𝐿𝐷L(D) power law (see Figure 15). Let us work this out in more detail.
paras.zh: 由于算力高效训练所使用的数据量随算力预算缓慢增长，L(Cmin) 预测的性能最终会触及由 L(D) 幂律设定的下界（见图 15）。让我们更详细地推导这一点。

paras.en: To keep overfitting under control, the results of Section 4 imply that we should scale the dataset size as
paras.zh: 为了控制过拟合，第 4 节的结果表明我们应该按以下方式缩放数据集大小：

paras.en: where we have used the compute-efficient N​(Cmin)𝑁subscript𝐶minN(C_{\rm min}) from Figure 14.
paras.zh: 其中我们使用了图 14 中算力高效的 N(Cmin)。

paras.en: Let us compare this to the data requirements of compute-efficient training. If we train at the critical batch size (i.e. C=2​Cmin𝐶2subscript𝐶minC=2C_{\rm min}) and never re-use data during training, we find that data usage grows with compute as
paras.zh: 让我们将其与算力高效训练的数据需求进行比较。如果我们在临界批量大小（即 C=2Cmin）下训练，并且在训练过程中从不重复使用数据，我们发现数据使用量随算力增长为：

paras.en: This is the maximum rate at which the dataset size can productively grow with compute, since it means that we are only training for a single epoch. But it grows the dataset much more slowly than in Equation (6.6). It appears to imply that compute-efficient training will eventually run into a problem with overfitting, even if the training process never re-uses any data!
paras.zh: 这是数据集大小随算力有效增长的最大速率，因为它意味着我们只训练一个 epoch。但它的数据集增长速度远低于方程 (6.6)。这似乎意味着，即使训练过程从不重复使用任何数据，算力高效训练最终也会遇到过拟合问题！

paras.en: According to Figure 1, we expect that when we are bottlenecked by the dataset size (ie by overfitting), the loss should scale as L​(D)∝D−0.095proportional-to𝐿𝐷superscript𝐷0.095L(D)\propto D^{-0.095}. This implies that the loss would scale with compute as L​(D​(Cmin))∝Cmin−0.03proportional-to𝐿𝐷subscript𝐶minsuperscriptsubscript𝐶min0.03L(D(C_{\rm min}))\propto C_{\rm min}^{-0.03} once we are data-limited. Once again, we have a contradiction, as this will eventually intersect with our prediction for L​(Cmin)𝐿subscript𝐶minL(C_{\rm min}) from Figure 13, where we found a scaling L​(Cmin)∝Cmin−0.050proportional-to𝐿subscript𝐶minsuperscriptsubscript𝐶min0.050L(C_{\rm min})\propto C_{\rm min}^{-0.050}.
paras.zh: 根据图 1，我们预期当受数据集大小（即过拟合）瓶颈限制时，损失应缩放为 L(D)∝D^{-0.095}。这意味着一旦我们受数据限制，损失随算力的缩放为 L(D(Cmin))∝Cmin^{-0.03}。我们再次面临矛盾，因为这将最终与我们从图 13 中得到的 L(Cmin) 预测相交，其中我们发现缩放为 L(Cmin)∝Cmin^{-0.050}。

paras.en: The intersection point of L​(D​(Cmin))𝐿𝐷subscript𝐶minL(D(C_{\rm min})) and L​(Cmin)𝐿subscript𝐶minL(C_{\rm min}) occurs at
paras.zh: L(D(Cmin)) 和 L(Cmin) 的交点出现在：

paras.en: though the numerical values are highly uncertain, varying by an order or magnitude in either direction depending on the precise values of the exponents from the power-law fits. The most obvious interpretation is that our scaling laws break down at or before we reach this point, which is still many orders of magnitude away in both compute and model size.
paras.zh: 尽管数值高度不确定，根据幂律拟合中指数的精确值，可能在一个数量级上下变化。最明显的解释是，我们的缩放定律在达到这一点之前或之时失效，而这一点在算力和模型规模上仍然相距多个数量级。

paras.en: One might also conjecture that this intersection point has a deeper meaning. If we cannot increase the model size beyond N∗superscript𝑁N^{*} without qualitatively different data requirements, perhaps this means that once we reach Cmin∗superscriptsubscript𝐶minC_{\rm min}^{*} and N∗superscript𝑁N^{*}, we have extracted all of the reliable information available in natural language data. In this interpretation, L∗superscript𝐿L^{*} would provide a rough estimate for the entropy-per-token666Defining words using the wc utility, the WebText2 dataset has 1.41.41.4 tokens per word and 4.34.34.3 characters per token. of natural language. In this scenario, we would expect the loss trend to level off at or before L∗superscript𝐿L^{*}.
paras.zh: 人们也可能猜想这个交点具有更深层的含义。如果我们不能在没有定性不同数据需求的情况下将模型规模增加到 N* 以上，也许这意味着一旦我们达到 Cmin* 和 N*，我们就已经提取了自然语言数据中所有可靠的信息。在这种解释中，L* 将提供自然语言每 token 熵的粗略估计。在这种情况下，我们预期损失趋势会在 L* 之前或之时趋于平稳。

paras.en: We can guess at the functional form of L​(Cmin)𝐿subscript𝐶minL(C_{\rm min}) as it levels off by considering a version of our training dataset with added noise. For example, we could append a random string of tokens to each context shown to the model to artificially boost the loss by a constant additive factor. Then, the distance from the noise floor L−Lnoise𝐿subscript𝐿noiseL-L_{\rm noise} would be a more meaningful performance metric, with even a small decrease in this distance potentially representing a significant boost in qualitative performance. Since the artificial noise would affect all of our trends equally, the critical point of 6.8 would not change (aside from the absolute value of L∗superscript𝐿L^{*}), and may be meaningful even if it occurs after the leveling off.
paras.zh: 我们可以通过考虑一个添加噪声的训练数据集版本来猜测 L(Cmin) 在趋于平稳时的函数形式。例如，我们可以向模型看到的每个上下文附加一个随机 token 字符串，以人为地增加一个常数加性因子的损失。那么，到噪声基底的距离 L - L_noise 将是一个更有意义的性能指标，即使这个距离的微小减少也可能代表定性性能的显著提升。由于人工噪声会同等影响我们所有的趋势，方程 6.8 的临界点不会改变（除了 L* 的绝对值），并且即使发生在趋于平稳之后也可能有意义。

paras.en: Power laws can arise from a wide variety of sources [THK18]. Power-law scalings with model and dataset size in density estimation [Was06] and in random forest models [Bia12] may be connected with our results. These models suggest that power-law exponents may have a very rough interpretation as the inverse of the number of relevant features in the data.
paras.zh: 幂律可能源于多种来源 [THK18]。密度估计 [Was06] 和随机森林模型 [Bia12] 中关于模型大小和数据集大小的幂律缩放可能与我们的结果相关。这些模型表明，幂律指数可能粗略地解释为数据中相关特征数量的倒数。

paras.en: Some early [BB01, Goo01] work found power-law scalings between performance and dataset size. More recent work [HNA+17, HAD19] also investigated scaling between model size and data size; their work is perhaps the closest to ours in the literature777After this work was completed, [RRBS19a] also appeared, which makes similar predictions for the dependence of loss on both model and dataset size.. Note, however, that [HNA+17] found super-linear scaling of dataset size with model size, whereas we find a sub-linear scaling. There are some parallels between our findings on optimal allocation of compute and [Kom19], including power-law learning curves. EfficientNets [TL19] also appear to obey an approximate power-law relation between accuracy and model size. Very recent work [RRBS19b] studies scaling with both dataset size and model size for a variety of datasets, and fits an ansatz similar to ours.
paras.zh: 一些早期工作 [BB01, Goo01] 发现性能与数据集大小之间存在幂律缩放。最近的工作 [HNA+17, HAD19] 也研究了模型大小与数据大小之间的缩放；它们的工作可能是文献中最接近我们的777在本工作完成后，[RRBS19a] 也出现了，它对损失随模型和数据集大小的依赖做出了类似的预测。然而，请注意，[HNA+17] 发现数据集大小随模型大小超线性缩放，而我们发现的是次线性缩放。我们在算力最优分配方面的发现与 [Kom19] 有一些相似之处，包括幂律学习曲线。EfficientNets [TL19] 似乎也遵循准确率与模型大小之间的近似幂律关系。最近的工作 [RRBS19b] 研究了多种数据集上随数据集大小和模型大小的缩放，并拟合了与我们类似的 ansatz。

paras.en: EfficientNet [TL19] advocates scaling depth and width exponentially (with different coefficients) for optimal performance of image models, resulting in a power-law scaling of width as a function of depth. We find that for language models this power should be roughly one when scaling up (as width/depth should remain fixed). But more importantly, we find that the precise architectural hyperparameters are unimportant compared to the overall scale of the language model. In [VWB16] it was argued that deep models can function as ensembles of shallower models, which could potentially explain this finding. Earlier work [ZK16] has compared width and depth, and found that wide ResNets can outperform deep ResNets on image classification. Some studies fix computation per data example, which tends to scale in proportion to the number of model parameters, whereas we investigate scaling with both model size and the quantity of training computation.
paras.zh: EfficientNet [TL19] 主张为了图像模型的最优性能，指数地缩放深度和宽度（使用不同的系数），导致宽度作为深度的函数呈幂律缩放。我们发现对于语言模型，在扩展时这个幂次应大致为 1（因为宽度/深度应保持固定）。但更重要的是，我们发现与语言模型的整体规模相比，精确的架构超参数并不重要。在 [VWB16] 中，有人认为深度模型可以作为浅层模型的集成，这可能解释这一发现。早期工作 [ZK16] 比较了宽度和深度，发现宽 ResNet 在图像分类上可以胜过深 ResNet。一些研究固定每个数据示例的计算量，这通常与模型参数数量成比例缩放，而我们研究的是随模型大小和训练计算量的缩放。

paras.en: Various works [AS17, BHMM18] have investigated generalization in highly overparameterized models, finding a “jamming transition” [GJS+19] when the model size reaches the dataset size (this may require training many orders of magnitude beyond typical practice, and in particular does not use early stopping). We do not observe such a transition, and find that the necessary training data scales sublinearly in the model size. Expansions in the model size, particularly at large width [JGH18, LXS+19], may provide a useful framework for thinking about some of our scaling relations. Our results on optimization, such as the shape of learning curves, can likely be explained using a noisy quadratic model, which can provide quite accurate predictions [ZLN+19] in realistic settings. Making this connection quantitative will require a characterization of the Hessian spectrum [Pap18, GKX19, GARD18].
paras.zh: 多项工作 [AS17, BHMM18] 研究了高度过参数化模型中的泛化，发现当模型大小达到数据集大小时会出现“堵塞转变” [GJS+19]（这可能需要训练比典型实践多几个数量级，并且特别是不使用早停）。我们没有观察到这样的转变，并且发现所需的训练数据随模型大小次线性缩放。模型大小的扩展，特别是在大宽度下 [JGH18, LXS+19]，可能为思考我们的一些缩放关系提供有用的框架。我们在优化方面的结果，例如学习曲线的形状，可能可以用噪声二次模型来解释，该模型在现实环境中可以提供相当准确的预测 [ZLN+19]。使这种联系定量化将需要 Hessian 谱的表征 [Pap18, GKX19, GARD18]。

paras.en: We have observed consistent scalings of language model log-likelihood loss with non-embedding parameter count N𝑁N, dataset size D𝐷D, and optimized training computation Cminsubscript𝐶minC_{\rm min}, as encapsulated in Equations (1.5) and (1.6). Conversely, we find very weak dependence on many architectural and optimization hyperparameters. Since scalings with N,D,Cmin𝑁𝐷subscript𝐶minN,D,C_{\rm min} are power-laws, there are diminishing returns with increasing scale.
paras.zh: 我们观察到语言模型的对数似然损失与非嵌入参数数量 N、数据集大小 D 以及优化训练计算量 C_min 之间存在一致的缩放关系，如公式 (1.5) 和 (1.6) 所示。相反，我们发现许多架构和优化超参数的影响非常微弱。由于与 N、D、C_min 的缩放关系是幂律的，因此随着规模的扩大，收益递减。

paras.en: We were able to precisely model the dependence of the loss on N𝑁N and D𝐷D, and alternatively on N𝑁N and S𝑆S, when these parameters are varied simultaneously. We used these relations to derive the compute scaling, magnitude of overfitting, early stopping step, and data requirements when training large language models. So our scaling relations go beyond mere observation to provide a predictive framework. One might interpret these relations as analogues of the ideal gas law, which relates the macroscopic properties of a gas in a universal way, independent of most of the details of its microscopic consituents.
paras.zh: 我们能够精确地对损失随 N 和 D 的变化关系进行建模，也可以对 N 和 S 同时变化时的关系进行建模。我们利用这些关系推导了训练大型语言模型时的计算缩放、过拟合程度、早停步骤和数据需求。因此，我们的缩放关系不仅仅是观察结果，而是提供了一个预测框架。可以将这些关系视为理想气体定律的类比，该定律以普遍的方式关联气体的宏观性质，而与其微观成分的细节无关。

paras.en: It is natural to conjecture that the scaling relations will apply to other generative modeling tasks with a maximum likelihood loss, and perhaps in other settings as well. To this purpose, it will be interesting to test these relations on other domains, such as images, audio, and video models, and perhaps also for random network distillation. At this point we do not know which of our results depend on the structure of natural language data, and which are universal. It would also be exciting to find a theoretical framework from which the scaling relations can be derived: a ‘statistical mechanics’ underlying the ‘thermodynamics’ we have observed. Such a theory might make it possible to derive other more precise predictions, and provide a systematic understanding of the limitations of the scaling laws.
paras.zh: 自然可以推测，这些缩放关系将适用于其他具有最大似然损失的生成式建模任务，或许也适用于其他设置。为此，测试这些关系在其他领域（如图像、音频和视频模型）以及随机网络蒸馏中的适用性将很有意义。目前我们尚不清楚哪些结果依赖于自然语言数据的结构，哪些是普适的。如果能找到一个可以从理论上推导出缩放关系的框架，即我们所观察到的“热力学”背后的“统计力学”，那将是令人兴奋的。这样的理论可能使我们能够推导出其他更精确的预测，并提供对缩放定律局限性的系统理解。

paras.en: In the domain of natural language, it will be important to investigate whether continued improvement on the loss translates into improvement on relevant language tasks. Smooth quantitative change can mask major qualitative improvements: “more is different”. For example, the smooth aggregate growth of the economy provides no indication of the specific technological developments that underwrite it. Similarly, the smooth improvements in language model loss may hide seemingly qualitative changes in capability.
paras.zh: 在自然语言领域，研究损失的持续改善是否能转化为相关语言任务的改进将非常重要。平滑的定量变化可能掩盖重大的定性改进：“多即不同”。例如，经济的平滑总体增长并未揭示支撑其发展的具体技术进展。类似地，语言模型损失的平滑改善可能隐藏着能力上看似质的变化。

paras.en: Our results strongly suggest that larger models will continue to perform better, and will also be much more sample efficient than has been previously appreciated. Big models may be more important than big data. In this context, further investigation into model parallelism is warranted. Deep models can be trained using pipelining [HCC+18], which splits parameters depth-wise between devices, but eventually requires increased batch sizes as more devices are used. Wide networks on the other hand are more amenable to parallelization [SCP+18], since large layers can be split between multiple workers with less serial dependency. Sparsity [CGRS19, GRK17] or branching (e.g. [KSH12]) may allow for even faster training of large networks through increased model parallelism. And using methods like [WRH17, WYL19], which grow networks as they train, it might be possible to remain on the compute-efficient frontier for an entire training run.
paras.zh: 我们的结果强烈表明，更大的模型将继续表现更好，并且样本效率将比之前认识到的要高得多。大模型可能比大数据更重要。在此背景下，进一步研究模型并行性是合理的。深度模型可以使用流水线并行 [HCC+18] 进行训练，该方法按深度方向在设备间分割参数，但随着使用更多设备，最终需要增加批量大小。另一方面，宽网络更适合并行化 [SCP+18]，因为大层可以在多个工作节点之间分割，序列依赖性较小。稀疏性 [CGRS19, GRK17] 或分支结构（例如 [KSH12]）可能通过增加模型并行性来实现更快的训练。使用像 [WRH17, WYL19] 这样的方法，在训练过程中逐步增长网络，可能使得在整个训练过程中保持在计算效率前沿。
